findings.title: Empty agent scripts/ folder matches documented delegation to quality-control
findings.detail: SKILL.md explicitly delegates deterministic checks (accessibility, brand validation, SQLite logging) to the companion quality-control skill and maps active integrations to accessibility_checker.py, brand_validator.py, and quality_logger.py. Placing those scripts here would duplicate ownership and contradict the separation between judgment-focused Quinn-R and machine-verifiable checks. Zero scripts in this agent directory is architecturally correct.

findings.title: Dimension scores and overall verdict are deterministic functions of finding severities
findings.detail: Determinism confidence: certain. Estimated token savings: 100-300 per report when the model re-derives the subtraction rules and thresholds from prose. Implementation complexity: trivial. Language: python. Could be prepass: no (runs after findings exist). Feeds scanner: n/a. Reusable across skills: yes (any structured QA report). Help pattern savings: script could expose --help for threshold overrides vs inlining tool_policies fields.
findings.action: Add a small post-processor (ideally under quality-control or shared tooling) that accepts structured findings + tool_policies thresholds and emits dimension_scores, pass/fail per dimension, and overall verdict JSON for the LLM to embed without recomputing arithmetic.

findings.title: First-run environment path checks are pure filesystem existence verification
findings.detail: Determinism confidence: high. Estimated token savings: under 100 per onboarding. Implementation complexity: trivial. Language: bash or python. Could be prepass: yes. Feeds scanner: n/a. Reusable across skills: yes. Help pattern savings: minimal.
findings.action: Optional one-shot verify_paths script (companion skill or project bootstrap) that checks style-bible, course_context.yaml, tool_policies.yaml, coordination.db, and skills/quality-control/ paths and emits a JSON readiness block for the LLM.

findings.title: Multi-artifact pairing and lesson-plan reference rules are largely pattern-checkable
findings.detail: Determinism confidence: moderate (IDs and cross-refs are syntactic; semantic alignment still needs LLM). Estimated token savings: 150-400 on multi-file reviews if a script pre-extracts pairing tokens and LP references. Implementation complexity: moderate. Language: python. Could be prepass: yes. Feeds scanner: script-opportunities-style prepass. Reusable across skills: yes for this course pipeline.
findings.action: Pre-pass script to extract declared artifact IDs, lesson-plan LP-{id} references, and cross-artifact pointers; output a compact inconsistency list for Quinn-R to judge only ambiguous cases.

findings.title: Access-boundary checks before file operations are allowlist/denylist logic
findings.detail: Determinism confidence: high for path classification given fixed rules in access-boundaries.md. Estimated token savings: light per tool call if enforced outside the model. Implementation complexity: moderate (must stay in sync with documented zones). Language: python. Could be prepass: no. Feeds scanner: n/a. Reusable across skills: yes if generalized.
findings.action: Consider a shared path-guard utility (not necessarily inside this agent folder) that validates proposed paths against the documented read/write/deny zones before execution.

assessments.delegation_documentation: SKILL.md External Skills table and review-protocol.md Step 1 route automation to quality-control (accessibility_checker.py, brand_validator.py, quality_logger.py).
assessments.companion_skill_note: quality-control scripts were not found under skills/quality-control/ in this workspace snapshot; SKILL.md degradation handling already covers automated check failures with judgment-only fallback.

summary.assessment: Zero scripts in bmad-agent-quality-reviewer is correct: deterministic checks are owned by the companion quality-control skill per SKILL.md. Residual LLM-tax is light except for explicit score/verdict math in feedback-format.md (best moved to a shared or quality-control post-processor). One medium-value postprocessing opportunity; three low-value prepass/validation helpers that belong in shared or companion tooling, not duplicated in this agent.
